name: BROKEN TOP CLUB MAINTENANCE
address: 367 SW MT WASHINGTON DR, BEND, OR 97702

fr.elements: Main Building; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area; Open Green Space; Pedestrian Walkways
fr.recommendations: To enhance emergency response efficiency on the property, ensuring that multiple access points are clearly marked and easily navigable could improve rapid entry and movement for responders. Regular maintenance of pedestrian walkways and vehicle parking areas might support smoother transitions between different zones on the property, facilitating the effective deployment of resources. Managing dense vegetation near access points and walkways may enhance visibility and working conditions, potentially reducing response times. Providing clear signage and maintaining unobstructed routes to the main building and around the open green space could also assist in effective positioning and staging of emergency equipment. These measures together could significantly support the overall emergency operations conducted on-site.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetated Perimeter; Scattered Shrubs; Tree Cluster; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetation Between Structures; Vegetated Access Route; Open Terrain; Dense Tree Canopy
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space through the strategic management of vegetation adjacent to the structure and between structures could improve property preparedness. Increasing the separation between scattered trees and reducing the density of scattered shrubs around the property may help reduce fuel continuity. Addressing the vegetated perimeter and managing tree clusters can contribute to creating effective fuel breaks. Maintaining an open terrain by preserving the existing open areas and reducing dense tree canopy concentrations would further support resilience. Clearing or thinning vegetation along access routes could improve firefighter access and facilitate suppression operations. Establishing and maintaining defensible space around the residence will contribute to long-term wildfire resilience, creating a more manageable landscape in the event of nearby wildfire activity.
